title: pelargonium littorale
intro: Pelargonium littorale, also known as the coastal pelargonium, is a species of flowering plant in the family Geraniaceae. It is a herb with medicinal properties, traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, coughs, and digestive problems. The plant is known to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and fighting off infections. The herbal preparations of Pelargonium littorale include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to alleviate symptoms and promote overall well-being.
